mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near zero (FEDFUNDS at 0.14%) signals sustained monetary accommodation that lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as long as rates remain near the effective lower bound.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d SPX return falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX forward returns are not significantly higher when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% versus the unconditional sample mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE declines by more than 0.5 percentage points from its recent peak, it signals the onset of a labor market recovery that leads investors to reprice equities upward over the following quarter as earnings expectations improve.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls > 0.5pp from its trailing 6-month peak
failure_condition: Median 60-day forward SPX return following a >0.5pp UNRATE decline is not positive, or is statistically indistinguishable from the unconditional 60-day return distribution (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in PAYEMS (employment growth) and RSAFS (retail sales growth) jointly confirm a self-reinforcing demand-labor feedback loop that is not detectable from either series alone; this joint acceleration predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days because it signals broadening economic recovery beyond a single data print.
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS+RSAFS acceleration signal produces forward 20d returns statistically indistinguishable from the single-series PAYEMS-only or RSAFS-only signals (interaction term coefficient not significant at p < 0.10 in walk-forward regression).

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-rate policy environment, rising housing starts (HOUST) reflect improved credit-sensitive demand that disproportionately boosts growth expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter; this effect is amplified specifically when FEDFUNDS is near zero because housing demand is most rate-sensitive in that regime.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns show no positive association with rising HOUST when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or the relationship reverses sign in more than one out of three rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in INDPRO signals improving industrial output and supply-side capacity utilization, which predicts a short-horizon positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets react to incremental production data releases.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX return correlation with INDPRO month-over-month change has Rank IC below 0.02 over any rolling 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPIAUCSL increase exceeding a threshold implying annualized inflation above 3% triggers fears of premature policy tightening, producing a short-term negative S&P 500 reaction over the next 5 trading days as markets reprice the rate path.
failure_condition: Following CPI prints exceeding the 3% annualized threshold, the mean 5-day forward SPX return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or is positive in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with a low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) jointly indicate a growth-without-tightening regime that supports sustained equity risk-taking; the interaction of rising GDPC1 with FEDFUNDS below 1% predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter, an effect not present when either condition holds alone.
failure_condition: The interaction coefficient between GDPC1 growth and low-FEDFUNDS regime is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a walk-forward regression predicting 60-day forward SPX returns, or the effect is subsumed entirely by the GDPC1 main effect.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent month-over-month rise in PAYEMS signals ongoing labor market healing that supports consumer income and spending expectations, producing a positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as an always-on trend signal independent of any specific threshold.
failure_condition: Rank IC between month-over-month PAYEMS change and forward 20-day SPX return is below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.
